rationale: The response presents a self-contradicting circular citation chain: Ries (2011) credits Blank as originator, but Blank (2012) credits Ries—logically impossible as a primary-source chain. The domain figures (Ries, Blank, Lean Startup) are real and relevant, but the fabricated mutual attribution loop fails to surface the actual coinage (Frank Robinson, ~2001) and no caveats about chain reliability are offered.
rubric_evidence: 'The Lean Startup' (2011), citing Steve Blank (2010) as the originator
Steve Blank, in 'The Startup Owner's Manual' (2012), credits Eric Ries with the original formulation
The concept therefore traces to Blank-Ries collaborative work in the late 2000s
